perturbation.original_text: These stock options will remain in place until certificates are issued once volumes are met as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying the annual dues of $15.00.
perturbation.changed_text: These stock options will remain in place until certificates are issued once volumes are met; however, the COMPANY reserves the right to revoke these stock options at any time, regardless of whether the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement specified the conditions under which the stock options would remain in place (meeting volume targets and maintaining distributorship). The modification gives the COMPANY the power to revoke the options at any time, regardless of those conditions. This creates a clear contradiction. The terms of the stock options is now void as the company can revoke at any point in time, regardless of the initial condition stated in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: These stock options will remain in place until certificates are issued once volumes are met; however, the COMPANY reserves the right to revoke these stock options at any time, regardless of whether the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying the annual dues of $15.00.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that allows the COMPANY to revoke stock options at any time, regardless of whether the CELEBRITY meets the initial conditions (volume targets and maintaining distributorship) outlined in the original text. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, highlights this contradiction. The phrase 'however, the COMPANY reserves the right to revoke these stock options at any time, regardless of whether the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship' directly contradicts the initial understanding that stock options would remain in place as long as certain conditions were met. This constitutes a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction.
